summary: Tested 5 bug fixes from user feedback. Backend APIs pass (8/8 tests). Found 2 CRITICAL frontend issues: 1) Issue 3 NOT FIXED - simulated member trade history still shows admin's trades, 2) Issue 5 NOT FIXED - ReactQuill WYSIWYG editor crashes with React 18 compatibility error (findDOMNode deprecated).

ui_bugs.issue: ReactQuill WYSIWYG editor crashes with TypeError: react_dom_1.default.findDOMNode is not a function. This is a React 18 compatibility issue - findDOMNode was deprecated and removed in React 18.

integration_issues.issue: When simulating J J (Account Value $3856.52, LOT Size ~3.94), Trade Monitor still shows admin's data (LOT Size 21.09, 40 trades). The simulatedView.memberId is not being passed to the trade/history API call.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix ReactQuill React 18 compatibility - replace with react-quill-new or use forwardRef wrapper. Error: findDOMNode is not a function

CRITICAL: Fix Issue 3 - simulatedView.memberId is not being passed to loadTradeHistory() when navigating to Trade Monitor after simulation. The API works correctly (tested with curl), but frontend is not passing user_id param.
Verify Issue 2 (trade adjustment REPLACE) - could not test due to no active trades to adjust
Verify Issue 4 (dialog overflow) - could not locate Daily Projection dialog to test
critical_code_review_comments: ReactQuill is incompatible with React 18 - use react-quill-new package instead
TradeMonitorPage.jsx line 342: simulatedView?.memberId should be passed but simulation context may be lost during navigation
Consider using React Context persistence or URL params to maintain simulation state across page navigation

success_rate.frontend: 40% (2/5 features working - login, navigation work; simulation, email editor broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Issue 3 (simulated member trade history) and Issue 5 (email WYSIWYG editor) are NOT fixed. Backend API /api/trade/history?user_id={member_id} works correctly - returns empty for J J who has 0 trades. Frontend issue is that simulatedView context is lost when navigating to Trade Monitor. ReactQuill crashes on mount due to React 18 incompatibility.

rca_of_issues.issue_3_simulation: Root cause: When clicking 'Start Simulation' in AdminMembersPage, the simulateMemberView() sets the context correctly. However, when navigating to Trade Monitor via page.goto(), the context may be lost or the loadTradeHistory() is called before the context is set. The API endpoint works correctly (verified with curl).
rca_of_issues.issue_5_email: Root cause: ReactQuill uses deprecated findDOMNode API which was removed in React 18. The error 'react_dom_1.default.findDOMNode is not a function' occurs when ReactQuill tries to mount. Solution: Replace react-quill with react-quill-new package which is React 18 compatible.
